items.final_wording: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

items.final_wording: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports listening, documenting needs and concerns, validating, assessing safety and recording support discussed without asserting abuse or imposing action.

items.rationale: The exact WHO first-line-support section supports private, patient-led documentation of immediate emotional, physical, safety, and support needs without inferring violence, coercing disclosure, or generating an action.

items.final_wording: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

source.title: Health care for women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence: a clinical handbook
source.jurisdiction: International guidance requiring UAE legal, safeguarding and referral adaptation
source.population: Women subjected to intimate partner violence or sexual violence; the handbook does not directly address people under 18 or men.
source.setting: Health-care encounters including first-line support, history, examination and documentation.
source.exclusions: Exact for survivor-centred first-line support, consent to documentation, recording complaints, symptoms, signs and injuries, and history or examination only when clinically performed. It is not authority for universal screening or automatic reporting in the UAE.

exact_location.heading: History and physical examination after sexual violence

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports respectful, consent-based history and head-to-toe examination documentation only when relevant and performed; sexual-assault care is not generalized to every domestic-violence presentation.

items.rationale: The exact history and examination section supports clinician-entered history and examination only when disclosure and consent make it appropriate; no intimate examination, assault, or injury finding is inferred.

items.final_wording: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

source.title: Management of Domestic Abuse
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation
source.population: Adults presenting to emergency care where domestic abuse is disclosed or clinically suspected; the text focuses primarily on women.
source.setting: Emergency department recognition, confidential disclosure, injury documentation and local safeguarding support.
source.exclusions: Exact only for confidential, clinician-led enquiry where suspicion exists, documentation at disclosure, injury documentation and recording child-welfare concerns. It is not used for universal screening or automatic police, shelter or referral action.

exact_location.heading: Summary and Recognition of Domestic Abuse

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

items.rationale: The exact recognition section supports clinician awareness of immediate safety, injury, children, vulnerability, and escalation concerns without treating a generic screening field as a positive finding.

items.final_wording: Supports asking permission to document and recording complaints, symptoms, signs, injuries and relevant history in the person’s words without interpretation.
